input.background: Event A represents cat ownership and event B represents dog ownership. We know that Pr(B | A) = 0.4286, Pr(¬A) = 1 - Pr(A) = 1 - 0.35 = 0.65, and Pr(B and ¬A) = 0.15. Using conditional probability, Pr(B | ¬A) = Pr(B and ¬A) / Pr(¬A) = 0.15 / 0.65 = 0.2308.
input.question: Using the total probability rule, calculate Pr(B).

meta_info.caption: Total probability rule (Pet ownership).
meta_info.section: 5.5  MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
